preamble: Aspiration is a phonetic process where a burst of air accompanies the release of a stop consonant. The shift from unaspirated [d] to aspirated [dʱ] is phonemically distinctive in many Indo-Aryan and other language families, representing a key phonological feature in consonant inventories.

examples.note: Phonemic contrast between unaspirated and aspirated voiced stop in Hindi.
Phonemic contrast between unaspirated and aspirated voiced stop in Sindhi.
